name: jcorp-nomad
description: An extremely compact offline media server for Movies, Shows, Books, and Music.
readme_summary: Jcorp Nomad is an open-source, offline media server built on an ESP32-S3, designed to stream movies, music, books, and shows without an internet connection. It creates a local Wi-Fi hotspot and serves content through a browser interface, making it ideal for travel, remote locations, or situations without internet access.
key_features: Offline media streaming (movies, music, books, shows)
Offline Wikipedia and ZIM archive support
primary_use_case: To provide a portable, self-contained offline media server for accessing and streaming various media types in environments without internet connectivity.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, compact thumbdrive, subtly labeled 'Jcorp Nomad', is plugged into a portable projector. The projector beam illuminates a vibrant, cozy scene in a remote wilderness setting, showing people enjoying movies, reading books, and listening to music. The projector's light beam is composed of flowing digital data streams, symbolizing the media being served. In the background, a small, glowing Wi-Fi symbol indicates a local network. The overall mood is one of freedom and accessibility, showcasing media enjoyment anywhere, anytime. The art style should be a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration with a warm, inviting color palette.

name: PolyUploader
description: Upload your files remotely to different hosts.
readme_summary: PolyUploader is an open-source JavaScript application that allows users to upload files to multiple remote hosting sites simultaneously. It supports linking personal API keys, managing upload history, creating profiles for automation, and offers features like link protection and bundling multiple host links into a single shareable link.
key_features: Detailed upload history with expiration status and delete options
Generate single sharing links for bundled host links
primary_use_case: To provide a unified and efficient way for users to upload files to a large number of file hosting services simultaneously, with added features for management and link security.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, stylized illustration of a central, friendly robot character labeled 'PolyUploader' holding a digital file. The robot is simultaneously sending copies of this file through multiple, distinct, colorful beams of light towards various cloud-shaped icons representing different file hosting services. Each cloud icon has a small, unique logo. Around the robot, a subtle, glowing interface shows upload progress bars and a single, consolidated link icon. The background is a clean, modern digital space with subtle geometric patterns. The overall mood is efficient, secure, and user-friendly, emphasizing the 'upload to many places at once' functionality. 3D isometric illustration style with bright, clean colors and clear iconography.

name: ESP32-Paxcounter
description: Wifi & BLE driven passenger flow metering with cheap ESP32 boards
readme_summary: The ESP32-Paxcounter is a project that uses ESP32 microcontrollers to count people by detecting WiFi and Bluetooth signals from mobile devices. It also supports environmental sensors and offers various data transmission options like LoRaWAN and MQTT, with a focus on privacy by not storing MAC addresses.
key_features: WiFi & Bluetooth signal detection for passenger flow metering
LoRaWAN enabled for long-range data transmission
Privacy-focused design (no persistent MAC address storage)
Multiple data output options: SD card, LoRaWAN, MQTT, Serial
Low power consumption with ESP32 deep sleep mode
Support for various ESP32 LoRa IoT boards
primary_use_case: Real-time passenger flow metering and environmental data collection using low-cost ESP32 devices, with an emphasis on privacy and flexible data transmission.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot standing at a busy intersection, holding a digital counter. The robot is subtly scanning the air, and small, glowing icons representing WiFi and Bluetooth signals are flowing towards its scanner. Successfully counted 'people' are represented by simple, abstract human silhouettes that are passing through a gentle, glowing archway. In the background, a subtle LoRaWAN signal wave emanates from the robot towards a distant, stylized network tower. The scene is bright and clean, with a focus on the robot and the flow of signals. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with soft, rounded edges and a vibrant, friendly color palette. The repository name 'ESP32-Paxcounter' is subtly integrated into a small digital display on the robot.

name: StableAnimator
description: [CVPR2025] We present StableAnimator, the first end-to-end ID-preserving video diffusion framework, which synthesizes high-quality videos without any post-processing, conditioned on a reference image and a sequence of poses.
readme_summary: StableAnimator is a novel end-to-end framework for identity-preserving human image animation using video diffusion models. It synthesizes high-quality videos directly from a reference image and a sequence of poses, without requiring any post-processing like face swapping or restoration.
key_features: Conditioned on reference image and sequence of poses
Global content-aware Face Encoder for identity consistency
Distribution-aware ID Adapter to prevent temporal interference
HJB equation-based optimization for enhanced face quality during inference
primary_use_case: Generating realistic and identity-consistent animated videos of humans from static images and pose sequences.
cover_image_prompt: A digital sculptor carefully chiseling a block of marble that represents a static portrait. As the sculptor works, the marble transforms into a dynamic, animated figure, perfectly mimicking the pose sequence shown on a nearby holographic screen. The sculptor's tools emit subtle digital particles, and small UI elements displaying 'ID Preservation' and 'Pose Alignment' float around the scene. The background is a clean, modern studio with soft, focused lighting. The overall mood is one of precise creation and transformation. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing the transition from static to dynamic while maintaining the subject's original identity.

name: core
description: A privacy-preserving Raspberry Pi home security camera that uses advanced end-to-end encryption.
readme_summary: Secluso is a privacy-focused, open-source home security camera system for Raspberry Pi. It offers end-to-end encrypted remote access to live video, alerts, and recordings, ensuring footage is not shared with cloud providers. The system emphasizes a quick 5-minute setup and reproducible builds for transparency and trust.
primary_use_case: To provide users with a private, secure, and self-hostable home security camera system that prioritizes data privacy through end-to-end encryption and avoids reliance on third-party cloud services.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant, stylized owl perched on a Raspberry Pi, its eyes glowing with a secure, end-to-end encrypted data stream. The owl is subtly guarding a cozy home interior, with a small, transparent shield projecting a live, unreadable video feed from the camera. In the background, a shadowy, generic cloud icon is being pushed away by the owl's protective aura. Small, abstract data particles flow securely from the Pi to a distant, stylized mobile phone icon, bypassing any central servers. The scene is rendered in a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration style with a warm, secure color palette, emphasizing privacy and local control.

name: NauEnginePublic
description: Nau Engine is a free open-source game engine that's designed to be versatile and accessible to everyone.
readme_summary: Nau Engine is a free, open-source, and versatile game engine built with C++. It allows for unrestricted use, including commercial purposes, and encourages community contributions through its open-source nature and accessible source code.
key_features: Permissive for educational and commercial use
primary_use_case: Developing and building video games with a flexible and accessible game engine.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, bustling cityscape constructed entirely from building blocks, representing a game world. In the center, a skilled architect (representing the Nau Engine) is effortlessly assembling these blocks with a digital blueprint in hand, showing the creation of new structures and characters. Small, glowing particles of code and data flow from the blueprint to the blocks, symbolizing the engine's functionality. The scene is bathed in warm, inviting light, with subtle UI elements and the 'Nau Engine' logo integrated into a nearby billboard. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing creativity and accessibility in game development.
